OUESTTON
Under the provisions of the Act, what identification is
required on a uriiling endorsing two candidates for the Indian
vailey Hospital Board sent out by other individuals when the
candiiates- have reviewed and approved the message?
CONCLUS ION
Under the provisions of the Act, when a mass ruailing is sent
at the behest of a candidate or candidates each candidate is
deeraed to be the sender and nust be identified on the outside of a
poiiti"uf 
mailing which consists of over 2oo substantially similar
pieces of nail sent out in a calendar month'
FACTS
onorbeforeoctoberll,lggo,severalindividualsinforned
vou thev eranted to mail a message to the registered voters of the
indian valley Hospital District consisting of about 1,30o post
.uia= .nao.sing you and Pat Norberg, candidates for the hospital
dj.strict. 
It l,,as anticipated that the total cost of the mailer
would not exceed s3oo, but it r.ras not knoun whether contributions
foi iir" urailing effort would be rnade directly to the endorsed
candidates or ihether postage and other mailing costs would be
paid directly to the vendors by other individuals' 
You and Pat
uorberg reviewed and approved the nessage'
ANALYS I S
Section 82015 of the Act defines the tern "contributionrr as:
... a payment. . . except to the extent that full and
adequate consideration is received unless it is
cleir from the surrounding circunstances that it is
not nade for political purPoses. An expenditure
made at the behest of a candidate, conmittee or
elected officer is a contribution to the candidate,
committee or elected officer unless fuIl and
adequate consideration i.s recej.ved for making the
expenditure...
(Enphasis added. )
By reguLation, the term t'Eade at the behest" has been defined
as a pa)atrent nade rrunder the control or at the direction of, in
cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with, or at
the request or suggestion of a candidate, controlled comnj.ttee,
official 
conmittee of a political party, or organization forned or
existing prinarily for political purposes.t, (Regulati.on 18215.)
You have indicated that you and Pat Norberg revi.ewed and approved
the nessage. Therefore, there was consultation, cooperation, and
coordination betrreen the individuals desiring to send out the
